context_values.response.implement:
Implemented the next approved `chain:pallet` slice as a Phase 1 restart scaffold. The pallet now compiles as a Myosu-owned core from [lib.rs](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM71KWW01SKCJTVS36V85KY5/worktree/crates/myosu-chain/pallets/game-solver/src/lib.rs#L20), with local `NetUid` / `Balance` / rate-limit types, preserved clean data structs, and minimal rate-limit storage in [lib.rs](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM71KWW01SKCJTVS36V85KY5/worktree/crates/myosu-chain/pallets/game-solver/src/lib.rs#L141). I also reduced the active module graph and replaced the broken subtensor carryovers with restart-safe placeholders in [rate_limiting.rs](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM71KWW01SKCJTVS36V85KY5/worktree/crates/myosu-chain/pallets/game-solver/src/utils/rate_limiting.rs#L5), [check_coldkey_swap.rs](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM71KWW01SKCJTVS36V85KY5/worktree/crates/myosu-chain/pallets/game-solver/src/guards/check_coldkey_swap.rs#L1), [math.rs](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM71KWW01SKCJTVS36V85KY5/worktree/crates/myosu-chain/pallets/game-solver/src/epoch/math.rs#L1), and the stripped module roots under `staking`, `subnets`, and `macros`. `Cargo.lock` was updated only to record the new `log` dependency in [Cargo.lock](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM71KWW01SKCJTVS36V85KY5/worktree/Cargo.lock#L1811).

Verification passed with `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/myosu-cargo-target`: `cargo check -p pallet-game-solver` succeeded, and `cargo test -p pallet-game-solver --lib` passed all 13 unit tests. I wrote the curated implementation artifacts in [implementation.md](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM71KWW01SKCJTVS36V85KY5/worktree/outputs/chain/pallet/implementation.md), [verification.md](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM71KWW01SKCJTVS36V85KY5/worktree/outputs/chain/pallet/verification.md), and [integration.md](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM71KWW01SKCJTVS36V85KY5/worktree/outputs/chain/pallet/integration.md). I did not hand-author `quality.md` or `promotion.md`, because the lane rules assign those to the Quality Gate and Review stages.
